docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: The AFSDK leverages cryptographic primitives and zero-trust design principles to enable secure, private communication and identity assertion between wallet users on-chain and off-chain. This specification provides a unified interface for applications integrating MatterFi's AFSDK and adaptation of Nym, a privacy-enhancing identity model.

docs.title: Critical Capabilities
docs.text: This system is designed to support the following critical capabilities:

docs.title: Self-Sovereign Identity
docs.text:
• Deterministic cryptographic seed (BIP39) for identity derivation
  • Encrypted off-chain communication using secure channels over WebSocket or HTTP
  • Verifiable credentials through structured Authority and ContactCredential chains

docs.title: Multimodal Signaling
docs.text:
• Dynamic fallback paths for delivery and recovery
  • KYC-compatible privacy enabling users to share verifiable data without compromising anonymity

docs.title: Target Audience
docs.text:
This specification targets:

  • Full-stack developers
  • Cryptographic engineers
  • Systems integrators building user-centric blockchain and privacy-first Web3 applications

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text: The framework provides a comprehensive solution for privacy-preserving identity management and secure messaging in decentralized applications, combining the best of cryptographic primitives with practical usability considerations.

docs.title: Error and Retry Handling
docs.text: This section outlines how the WalletSDK and signaling infrastructure handle delivery failures, decryption errors, credential mismatches, and network interruptions to preserve integrity, confidentiality, and liveness of message delivery and payment execution.

docs.title: Error Categories
docs.text: Category Description Examples Network Errors Connectivity issues Timeout, DNS failure, connection reset Authentication Errors Token/session issues Expired token, invalid signature Decryption Errors Cryptographic failures Invalid key, corrupted ciphertext Protocol Errors Message format issues Invalid payload structure, version mismatch Resource Errors System limitations Rate limiting, storage full Severity Impact Response Critical Service unavailable Immediate alert, manual intervention High Feature degradation Automatic retry with fallback Medium Partial functionality Degraded mode operation Low Minor inconvenience Log and continue

docs.title: Retry Policy
docs.text:
• Transient Network Errors: Retries every 3 seconds, max 5 attempts
  • Decryption Failures: No retry, log for manual review
  • Token Expiry: Prompt re-authentication before reconnect
  • Invalid Signature or Credential: Fail-fast and flag signal

docs.title: SDK Event Hooks
docs.text:
Developers may respond to key retry events:

interface RetryEventHandlers {
  onRetryAttempt?: (attempt: number, maxAttempts: number, error: Error) => void;
  onRetryExhausted?: (finalError: Error, totalAttempts: number) => void;
  onFallbackActivated?: (originalStrategy: string, fallbackStrategy: string) => void;
  onRecoverySuccess?: (recoveryTime: number, strategyUsed: string) => void;
}

docs.title: Signal Backup and Recovery Protocol
docs.text:
Signal reliability is critical in a decentralized, peer-to-peer wallet environment. This protocol ensures message delivery, backup, and recovery across various fault domains (offline peers, smart contract congestion, temporary Nym/Notary downtime).

Each outbound signal is handled using a multi-path broadcast strategy combined with local caching:

Path Description Reliability Privacy Primary Nym Route Onion routing through mixnet High Maximum DeFi Beacon Backup On-chain signal storage Maximum Medium Direct P2P Fallback Peer-to-peer connection Variable Low Encrypted Local Cache Device storage backup High Maximum

If a signal fails to reach its destination via one method, it is retried via alternate paths automatically using a priority system.

docs.title: Outbox & Retry Queue (WalletSDK)
docs.text:
The WalletSDK maintains a local queue of signals that:

  • Were not acknowledged via ack
  • Were not retrieved within ACK_TIMEOUT

docs.title: Retry Logic Example
docs.text:
Retries prioritize:

  1. Nym if online
  2. DeFi beacon path
  3. Direct SDK peer-to-peer if contact is reachable
class SignalRetryManager {
  private retryQueue: SignalRetryItem[] = [];

  async enqueueForRetry(signal: SignalMessage, error: Error): Promise<void> {
    const retryItem: SignalRetryItem = {
      signal,
      error,
      attempts: 0,
      nextRetryAt: this.calculateNextRetryTime(0),
      strategies: this.getAvailableStrategies(signal)
    };

    this.retryQueue.push(retryItem);
    await this.persistRetryQueue();
  }

  private calculateNextRetryTime(attempt: number): Date {
    // Exponential backoff: 3s, 9s, 27s, 81s, 243s
    const delayMs = Math.pow(3, attempt) * 1000;
    return new Date(Date.now() + delayMs);
  }
}

docs.title: Signal Expiry & Garbage Collection
docs.text:
Signals are tagged with:

  • Creation timestamp
  • TTL (time-to-live) value
  • Priority level
  • Retry count

WalletSDK periodically runs cleanup jobs that remove expired, unacknowledged signals from the outbox.

interface SignalMetadata {
  id: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  ttl: number; // seconds
  priority: 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'urgent';
  retryCount: number;
  maxRetries: number;
}

class SignalGarbageCollector {
  async collectExpiredSignals(): Promise<void> {
    const expiredSignals = await this.findExpiredSignals();
    for (const signal of expiredSignals) {
      await this.notifySender(signal);
      await this.removeFromOutbox(signal);
    }
  }

  private async findExpiredSignals(): Promise<SignalMetadata[]> {
    const now = Date.now();
    return this.signalStore.find({
      $where: (signal) => {
        const expiryTime = signal.createdAt.getTime() + (signal.ttl * 1000);
        return now > expiryTime;
      }
    });
  }
}

docs.title: DeFi-Based Redundancy
docs.text:
All beaconed signals are also written on-chain using:

function storeSignal(
    bytes32 signalId,
    address recipient,
    bytes calldata encryptedSignal,
    bytes calldata ephemeralPubKey
) external {
    // Store encrypted signal on-chain for redundancy
    signals[signalId] = Signal({
        recipient: recipient,
        encryptedData: encryptedSignal,
        ephemeralPubKey: ephemeralPubKey,
        timestamp: block.timestamp,
        isDelivered: false
    });
}

Receiving wallets can:

  • Query smart contract logs for missed beacons
  • Use ephemeralPubKey to decrypt the signal retroactively
  • Sync missed message history post-reconnect

docs.title: Offline Peer Recovery
docs.text:
If the recipient wallet is offline, upon next boot:

  1. SDK queries last known DeFi signal anchors
  2. Attempts ECDH decryption of beacons
  3. Triggers normal message receipt if match is found
class OfflineRecoveryManager {
  async performRecovery(): Promise<void> {
    const lastKnownBlock = await this.getLastProcessedBlock();
    const missedSignals = await this.queryMissedSignals(lastKnownBlock);

    for (const signal of missedSignals) {
      try {
        const decryptedSignal = await this.attemptDecryption(signal);
        if (decryptedSignal) {
          await this.processDecryptedSignal(decryptedSignal);
        }
      } catch (error) {
        console.warn('Failed to decrypt missed signal:', error);
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: API Endpoints
docs.text: The following section defines the core HTTP and WebSocket API endpoints required to interface with the AFSDK, Credential Manager, & Signal Server. All endpoints are assumed to be authenticated via token/session key exchange unless otherwise noted.

docs.title: POST /identity/register
docs.text:
SDK Method Reference: register_name(sfname, paycode)

  • Implicit signature handling via get_my_pubkey() & sign_challenge()
  • Content-Type: application/json

Backend Action: Upon receiving this request:

  1. The backend validates the signature using the provided pubkey.
  2. It checks that sfname is available and not registered.
  3. It records the following new identity entry in the backend:
{
  "sfname": "alice.wallet",
  "pubkey": "0x...",
  "paycode": "PM8TJ...",
  "registered_at": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z",
  "status": "pending"
}
  1. Then, it emits a write to the DeFi SignalIdentity contract to finalize the registration.
  2. Once confirmation is received from the DeFi contract (via an emitted IdentityRegistered event), the backend updates the record:

Response:

{
  "success": true,
  "sfname": "alice.wallet",
  "tx_hash": "0x..."
}

docs.title: Authentication Flow
docs.text: Authentication in the AFSDK & Nym system ensures secure access to decentralized identity and messaging endpoints. The authentication model combines local key derivation, session tokens, and signed credentials for user identity assurance.

docs.title: Session Initialization
docs.text:
Upon wallet import or recovery:

  • The WalletSDK derives the master keypair from the mnemonic seed (BIP39).
  • The SDK initiates a session token using the derived public key.
  • The server verifies the signature from the private key against the payload (timestamp or nonce).

Flow:

  1. Client imports seed phrase.
  2. WalletSDK derives seckp256k1 keypair.
  3. Client signs a nonce or timestamp.
  4. Server verifies signature.
  5. Server issues sessionToken (JWT or HMAC).
  6. Client begins authenticated API interactions.

docs.title: Secure Header Token Structure
docs.text:
All subsequent HTTP requests must include:

Authorization: Bearer <sessionToken>

If the token is missing, expired, or invalid, the request is rejected with:

{
  "error": "Unauthorized",
  "message": "Invalid or expired token",
  "code": 401
}

docs.title: WebSocket Connection Handshake
docs.text:
For real-time messaging:

  • Client initiates a secure WebSocket connection.
  • The sessionToken is sent as part of the handshake headers.
  • Server validates the token and allows encrypted message exchange.

WebSocket Headers:

Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: afsdk-protocol
Authorization: Bearer <sessionToken>

Success Response:

{
  "type": "handshake_success",
  "session_id": "string",
  "user_sfname": "alice.wallet"
}

If token is invalid or sfname is mismatched:

{
  "type": "handshake_failed",
  "error": "Invalid credentials",
  "code": 1008
}

docs.title: Token Refresh
docs.text:
Clients must refresh tokens before expiration to avoid service interruptions. Tokens are valid for 60 minutes by default. Token refresh occurs proactively at 80% lifespan.

Endpoint: POST /auth/refresh

Payload:

{
  "current_token": "string"
}

Response:

{
  "success": true,
  "new_token": "string",
  "expires_at": "2025-08-18T11:00:00Z"
}

If token is expired or malformed:

{
  "error": "Token refresh failed",
  "message": "Token expired",
  "code": 401
}

docs.title: Revocation and Logout
docs.text:
Client sessions may be manually revoked by the user or automatically invalidated by the backend due to:

  • Logout request
  • Token reuse or forgery
  • Server-side rotation policy

Endpoint: POST /auth/revoke

Payload:

{
  "reason": "user_logout"
}

Response:

{
  "success": true,
  "revoked_at": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z"
}

After revocation, all API endpoints will return 401 Unauthorized until re-authentication.

docs.title: Signature Verification Logic (Pseudocode)
docs.text:
function verifySignature(publicKey, message, signature) {
  try {
    const isValid = secp256k1.verify(message, signature, publicKey);
    if (!isValid) {
      throw new Error('Invalid signature');
    }
    return true;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new AuthenticationError('Signature verification failed');
  }
}

docs.title: Authentication Failure Scenarios
docs.text: Scenario Error Code HTTP Status Description Invalid Token 401 401 Token malformed or expired Signature Mismatch 401 401 Public key doesn't match signature Rate Limited 429 429 Too many authentication attempts Account Suspended 403 403 User account temporarily suspended Network Timeout 408 408 Authentication server unreachable Invalid sfname 404 404 sfname not found in registry Expired Certificate 401 401 Client certificate expired

docs.title: Socket Parameters & Signal Routing
docs.text: WebSocket-based signaling provides real-time, low-latency communication between verified identities. This section defines the socket initialization parameters, reconnection logic, signal packet formats, and dynamic routing strategies via Nym.

docs.title: WebSocket Initialization Parameters
docs.text:
The client initiates a secure socket connection with the following headers:

GET /signal HTTP/1.1
Host: api.wallet-signal.io
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Key: dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==
Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13
Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: afsdk-signal-v1
Authorization: Bearer <sessionToken>

docs.title: Handshake Response Format
docs.text:
Upon successful connection:

{
  "type": "handshake_success",
  "session_id": "ws_session_12345",
  "user_sfname": "alice.wallet",
  "supported_protocols": ["afsdk-signal-v1"],
  "heartbeat_interval": 30000
}

docs.title: Signal Packet Structure
docs.text:
Each signal packet is a signed envelope containing metadata and encrypted content.

interface SignalPacket {
  type: 'signal' | 'ack' | 'error';
  id: string;
  timestamp: number;
  sender: string;
  recipient: string;
  routing: RoutingInfo;
  payload: string; // Base64 encrypted content
  signature: string; // Base64 signature
}

interface RoutingInfo {
  strategy: string;
  priority: 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'urgent';
  ttl: number; // Time to live in seconds
  fallback_allowed: boolean;
}

docs.title: Dynamic Routing Logic
docs.text:
Signal routing may dynamically select from available channels:

Channel Description Latency Privacy Level Nym Route Onion routing through mixnet High Maximum Direct WebSocket Peer-to-peer connection Low Medium Beacon Broadcast On-chain signaling Variable High Backup Relay Encrypted storage High Maximum Hybrid Fallback Multi-channel approach Variable Adaptive

docs.title: Reconnection Policy
docs.text:
If the socket connection fails or times out:

  • SDK attempts automatic reconnection every 3 seconds (up to 5 times)
  • SessionToken is reused unless expired
  • Event onReconnected is triggered for app-layer hooks
class WebSocketManager {
  private reconnectAttempts = 0;
  private maxReconnectAttempts = 5;
  private reconnectInterval = 3000;

  private async attemptReconnect(): Promise<void> {
    if (this.reconnectAttempts >= this.maxReconnectAttempts) {
      throw new Error('Max reconnection attempts exceeded');
    }

    this.reconnectAttempts++;
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, this.reconnectInterval));

    try {
      await this.connect();
      this.reconnectAttempts = 0;
      this.emit('reconnected');
    } catch (error) {
      this.attemptReconnect();
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Disconnection Scenarios
docs.text: Scenario Cause Recovery Action Timeout Network Loss Connectivity issues Auto-reconnect 30s Server Restart Maintenance Exponential backoff 60s Token Expiry Session timeout Token refresh + reconnect 5s Rate Limiting Too many requests Wait + retry 10s Protocol Error Invalid message format Manual reconnection N/A

docs.title: Fallback Routing Diagnostics
docs.text:
When primary signal routing fails, fallback logic is triggered based on routing priority. The following diagnostics are collected to trace and debug these scenarios:

  • Attempted Route: Channel attempted (e.g., nym, direct)
  • Failure Reason: Reason reported by transport layer (e.g., timeout, signature invalid)
  • Next Route Selected: Channel selected for retry
  • Timestamped Logs: Each fallback transition is logged with UTC timestamps

Example Fallback Log (JSON Format):

{
  "timestamp": "2025-08-18T10:05:23Z",
  "signal_id": "sig_12345",
  "attempted_route": "nym",
  "failure_reason": "timeout",
  "next_route": "direct_websocket",
  "retry_count": 1,
  "total_latency_ms": 15000
}

These logs are stored locally in encrypted form and optionally synced to secure diagnostic storage with user permission.

docs.title: Logging Strategy Levels
docs.text:
Signal diagnostics are logged according to severity:

Level Description Example Triggers ERROR Critical failures requiring immediate attention Connection completely lost WARN Potential issues that may affect performance High latency detected INFO Normal operational events Route switching successful DEBUG Detailed diagnostic information Strategy evaluation details TRACE Very detailed execution flow Individual packet routing

Logging can be enabled with verbosity filters at runtime using SDK options:

const loggingConfig = {
  level: 'info',
  filters: {
    include: ['routing', 'websocket'],
    exclude: ['heartbeat']
  },
  remoteSync: true,
  retentionDays: 30
};

These diagnostics ensure observability for decentralized message delivery while preserving user privacy and maintaining system integrity.

docs.title: Appendix A: Mock Environment Configurations
docs.text: This mock environment reflects the runtime behavior of the full WalletSDK system within a non-production testnet or partner-integration sandbox. It enables developers to validate session bootstrapping, encrypted signal propagation, and credential issuance workflows in conditions that mirror production reliability while offering extended observability.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• End-to-end Nym routing simulation
  • Mock token issuance via AuthService
  • Staging relay routing with metrics enabled
  • Credential Authority reachable over HTTP(S)
  • Wallet API connected to testnet nodes and ephemeral storage

These mocks support end-user test flows like:

  1. Importing a mnemonic and generating a full identity structure
  2. Issuing and verifying ContactCredentials
  3. Sending and receiving encrypted signals
  4. Simulating delivery timeouts and retry logic
  5. Validating session lifecycle and WebSocket fallbacks

docs.title: Configuration Parameters
docs.text: This configuration simulates a live testing environment used during pre-production validation of WalletSDK components and Nym communication layers. Parameters are tuned to enable verbose logging, stateless API tests, and fault injection support.

docs.title: Appendix B: Credential Schema Example
docs.text:
ContactCredentials function as cryptographic assertions of identity within the MatterFi/Nym trust domain. These credentials are signed at the moment of creation and can be verified both locally and remotely using the issuer's public key.

This credential schema supports both human-readable and machine-verifiable identity structures.

docs.title: Credential Field Breakdown
docs.text:
• issuedTo: Identifier of the recipient Nym or contact (typically a .k alias)
  • issuedBy: Credential authority or peer (another WalletSDK user)
  • issuedAt: ISO 8601 timestamp (RFC3339 compliant)
  • role: Defines credential context, such as CONTACT, TRUSTED_NODE, or VERIFIED_MINTER
  • metadata: Arbitrary JSON structure; may include avatar, publicKey, label, or externally verifiable claims
  • signature: Base64-encoded ED25519 digital signature over a canonicalized credential hash

docs.title: Complete Credential Example
docs.text:
{
  "type": "ContactCredential",
  "version": "1.0",
  "id": "cred_1234567890abcdef",
  "issuedTo": {

    "nymAddress": "nym://alice@relay.nymtech.net"
  },

    "authorityLevel": "trusted"
  },

  },
  "proof": {
    "type": "Ed25519Signature2020",
    "created": "2025-08-18T10:00:00.000Z",
    "verificationMethod": "did:nym:nym-authority#key-1",

  }

docs.title: Credential Verification Steps
docs.text:
1. Parse the payload and extract the signature field
  2. Canonicalize the JSON fields (excluding the signature)
  3. Compute the hash and verify against the public key of issuedBy
  4. Confirm credential hasn't been revoked (if revocation registry is enabled)

docs.title: Verification Implementation
docs.text:
async function verifyCredential(credential: ContactCredential): Promise<boolean> {
  try {
    // Step 1: Extract signature and remove from payload for verification
    const { proof, ...credentialWithoutProof } = credential;

    // Step 2: Canonicalize the JSON (deterministic serialization)
    const canonicalized = canonicalizeJson(credentialWithoutProof);

    // Step 3: Compute hash
    const credentialHash = sha256(canonicalized);

    // Step 4: Verify signature
    const signatureValid = await verifyEd25519Signature(
      credentialHash,
      proof.proofValue,
      credential.issuedBy.publicKey
    );

    if (!signatureValid) {
      return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Check expiry
    const now = new Date();
    const expiresAt = new Date(credential.expiresAt);

    if (now > expiresAt) {
      return false;
    }

    // Step 6: Check revocation status (if registry available)
    const isRevoked = await checkRevocationStatus(credential.id);
    if (isRevoked) {
      return false;
    }

    return true;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Credential verification failed:', error);
    return false;
  }
}

function canonicalizeJson(obj: any): string {
  // RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization
  return JSON.stringify(obj, Object.keys(obj).sort());
}

docs.title: Integration with DID Systems
docs.text:
This format aligns with the OpenTransactions-Nym approach and supports embedding into WalletSDK sessions, DIDs, or encrypted communications.

The schema shows a verifiable credential (ContactCredential) issued from one Nym identity to another. Signatures and issuedBy fields can be verified using the public keys cached or fetched from the authority.

Key integration points:

  • issuedTo and issuedBy: Resolved aliases or unique wallet handles
  • issuedAt: RFC3339 timestamp
  • metadata: Encrypted or plain auxiliary data (optional)
  • signature: ED25519 signature over deterministic hash of credential

docs.title: Appendix C: Message Payload Example
docs.text:
Signal messages serve as the foundational transport layer for encrypted communication between WalletSDK clients. All message types whether signal, ack, or error inherit from this base payload structure. The envelope supports routing metadata, authenticated encryption, and message tracing for observability.

Encryption is handled using AES-GCM with keys derived from ephemeral X25519 sessions established at initial peer handshake. Signatures ensure origin authentication and tamper protection.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Nym-routed encrypted chat messages
  • Identity proofs or QR-based handshakes
  • Payment triggers or confirmations
  • Out-of-band session refresh notifications

docs.title: Base Message Structure
docs.text:
interface BaseMessage {
  id: string;
  type: 'signal' | 'ack' | 'error' | 'handshake';
  version: string;
  timestamp: string; // RFC3339
  sender: string; // sfname
  recipient?: string; // sfname (optional for broadcasts)
  ttl?: number; // seconds
  priority?: 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'urgent';
}

docs.title: Complete Signal Message Example
docs.text:
{

  "type": "signal",

  "timestamp": "2025-08-18T10:30:45.123Z",
  "sender": "alice.wallet",
  "recipient": "bob.wallet",
  "ttl": 3600,
  "priority": "normal",
  "routing": {
    "strategy": "nym",
    "fallback_allowed": true,
    "max_hops": 5,
    "anonymity_level": "high"
  },
  "encryption": {
    "algorithm": "AES-GCM-256",
    "key_derivation": "HKDF-SHA512",
    "ephemeral_public_key": "0x0279BE667EF9DCBBAC55A06295CE870B07029BFCDB2DCE28D959F2815B16F81798",
    "nonce": "0x1234567890abcdef12345678"
  },
  "payload": "base64_encoded_encrypted_content_here...",
  "signature": "base64_encoded_ed25519_signature_here...",
  "metadata": {
    "message_type": "text",
    "content_length": 256,
    "compression": "none",
    "checksum": "sha256_hash_of_plaintext"
  }
}

docs.title: Message Field Breakdown
docs.text:
• type: Signal type (e.g., signal, ack, error)
  • routing: Channel prioritization (e.g., nym, direct, store-and-forward)
  • payload: Base64 AES-GCM-encrypted content
  • signature: Base64 ED25519 signature for verification

docs.title: Key Exchange and Derivation
docs.text:
async function establishEncryptionContext(
  senderPrivateKey: Uint8Array,
  recipientPublicKey: Uint8Array
): Promise<EncryptionContext> {
  // Generate ephemeral keypair for this message
  const ephemeralKeypair = await generateX25519Keypair();

  // Perform Diffie-Hellman key exchange
  const sharedSecret = await x25519(ephemeralKeypair.privateKey, recipientPublicKey);

  // Derive encryption key using HKDF
  const encryptionKey = await hkdf(
    sharedSecret,
    'message-encryption',
    32, // 256 bits
    'sha512'
  );

  return {
    ephemeralPublicKey: ephemeralKeypair.publicKey,
    encryptionKey,
    nonce: generateRandomNonce()
  };
}

docs.title: Message Encryption
docs.text:
async function encryptMessage(
  plaintext: string,
  context: EncryptionContext
): Promise<EncryptedPayload> {
  // Serialize message with metadata
  const messageData = {
    content: plaintext,
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    sender: context.sender
  };

  const serialized = JSON.stringify(messageData);

  // Encrypt using AES-GCM
  const ciphertext = await aesGcmEncrypt(
    serialized,
    context.encryptionKey,
    context.nonce
  );

  // Sign the ciphertext
  const signature = await ed25519Sign(
    ciphertext,
    context.senderPrivateKey
  );

  return {
    ephemeralPublicKey: context.ephemeralPublicKey,
    nonce: context.nonce,
    ciphertext: base64Encode(ciphertext),
    signature: base64Encode(signature)
  };
}

docs.title: Message Decryption
docs.text:
async function decryptMessage(
  encryptedPayload: EncryptedPayload,
  recipientPrivateKey: Uint8Array,
  senderPublicKey: Uint8Array
): Promise<string> {
  // Verify signature first
  const signatureValid = await ed25519Verify(
    base64Decode(encryptedPayload.ciphertext),
    base64Decode(encryptedPayload.signature),
    senderPublicKey
  );

  if (!signatureValid) {
    throw new Error('Invalid message signature');
  }

  // Derive shared secret
  const sharedSecret = await x25519(
    recipientPrivateKey,
    encryptedPayload.ephemeralPublicKey
  );

  // Derive decryption key
  const decryptionKey = await hkdf(
    sharedSecret,
    'message-encryption',
    32,
    'sha512'
  );

  // Decrypt ciphertext
  const decrypted = await aesGcmDecrypt(
    base64Decode(encryptedPayload.ciphertext),
    decryptionKey,
    encryptedPayload.nonce
  );

  // Parse and validate message
  const messageData = JSON.parse(decrypted);

  // Additional validation
  if (messageData.timestamp) {
    const messageTime = new Date(messageData.timestamp);
    const now = new Date();
    const age = now.getTime() - messageTime.getTime();

    // Reject messages older than 1 hour
    if (age > 3600000) {
      throw new Error('Message too old');
    }
  }

  return messageData.content;
}

docs.title: Schema Validation
docs.text:
function validateMessage(message: any): boolean {
  const requiredFields = ['id', 'type', 'version', 'timestamp', 'sender'];
  const hasRequiredFields = requiredFields.every(field => field in message);

  if (!hasRequiredFields) {
    return false;
  }

  // Validate timestamp format
  if (!isValidRFC3339(message.timestamp)) {
    return false;
  }

  // Validate sfname format
  if (!isValidSfname(message.sender)) {
    return false;
  }

  // Type-specific validation
  switch (message.type) {
    case 'signal':
      return validateSignalMessage(message);
    case 'ack':
      return validateAckMessage(message);
    case 'error':
      return validateErrorMessage(message);
    default:
      return false;
  }
}

docs.title: Security Validation
docs.text:
async function validateMessageSecurity(message: SignalMessage): Promise<boolean> {
  // Check for replay attacks
  const isReplay = await checkReplayCache(message.id);
  if (isReplay) {
    return false;
  }

  // Verify signature
  const signatureValid = await verifyMessageSignature(message);
  if (!signatureValid) {
    return false;
  }

  // Check message age
  const messageAge = Date.now() - new Date(message.timestamp).getTime();
  if (messageAge > MAX_MESSAGE_AGE_MS) {
    return false;
  }

  // Rate limiting check
  const withinRateLimit = await checkRateLimit(message.sender);
  if (!withinRateLimit) {
    return false;
  }

  return true;
}

docs.title: Appendix D: Acknowledgment (Ack) Schema
docs.text:
Acknowledgment messages form the backbone of signal delivery confirmation. They are sent automatically in response to a valid, decryptable, and authenticated incoming message.

Acks serve to:

  • Confirm delivery and routing success
  • Prevent retries for confirmed messages
  • Aid clients in signaling liveliness
  • Provide metadata for latency and diagnostics

Optional extensions to this schema may include:

  • Signature of the original message hash for integrity confirmation
  • Forwarding chain identifier (in store-and-forward flows)
  • Relayed timestamp (from intermediary node)

For sensitive messages (e.g., credential issuance or payments), the status may elevate to verified only after backend or authority validation.

Acknowledgments notify the sender that their signal was received, decrypted, and accepted for processing. This schema supports delivery monitoring and prevents retry loops.

docs.title: Base Acknowledgment Structure
docs.text:
interface BaseAcknowledgment {
  id: string;
  type: 'ack';
  version: string;
  timestamp: string; // RFC3339
  originalMessageId: string;
  sender: string; // sfname of ack sender
  recipient: string; // sfname of original sender
  status: 'delivered' | 'verified' | 'pending' | 'failed';

docs.title: Sender-Side Processing
docs.text:
class AcknowledgmentProcessor {
  private pendingAcknowledgments = new Map<string, AcknowledgmentPromise>();

  async sendMessage(message: SignalMessage): Promise<DeliveryResult> {
    const deliveryPromise = new AcknowledgmentPromise(message.id);
    this.pendingAcknowledgments.set(message.id, deliveryPromise);

    // Send message
    await this.signalTransport.send(message);

    // Set timeout for acknowledgment
    const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {
      setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Acknowledgment timeout')), ACK_TIMEOUT_MS);
    });

    try {
      const acknowledgment = await Promise.race([
        deliveryPromise,
        timeoutPromise
      ]);

      return this.processAcknowledgment(acknowledgment);
    } catch (error) {
      // Handle timeout or other errors
      return this.handleAcknowledgmentFailure(message, error);
    }
  }

  processAcknowledgment(ack: Acknowledgment): DeliveryResult {
    switch (ack.status) {
      case 'delivered':
        return { status: 'delivered', latency: this.calculateLatency(ack) };
      case 'verified':
        return { status: 'verified', latency: this.calculateLatency(ack) };
      case 'failed':
        return { status: 'failed', error: ack.error_details };
      case 'pending':
        return { status: 'pending', estimatedCompletion: ack.processing_metadata?.estimated_completion_time };
      default:
        return { status: 'unknown' };
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Recipient-Side Generation
docs.text:
async function generateAcknowledgment(
  originalMessage: SignalMessage,
  processingResult: ProcessingResult
): Promise<Acknowledgment> {
  const baseAck: BaseAcknowledgment = {
    id: generateAcknowledgmentId(),
    type: 'ack',
    version: '1.0',
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    original_message_id: originalMessage.id,
    sender: getCurrentIdentity().sfname,
    recipient: originalMessage.sender,
    status: processingResult.status
  };

  // Add processing metadata
  const processingMetadata = {
    received_at: processingResult.receivedAt,
    decrypted_at: processingResult.decryptedAt,
    processed_at: processingResult.processedAt,
    processing_time_ms: processingResult.processingTime,
    routing_strategy_used: processingResult.routingStrategy,
    hops_taken: processingResult.hopsTaken
  };

  // Add validation results
  const validationResults = {
    signature_verified: processingResult.signatureValid,
    decryption_successful: processingResult.decryptionSuccessful,
    content_integrity: processingResult.integrityCheck,
    replay_check: processingResult.replayCheck
  };

  // Add delivery confirmation
  const deliveryConfirmation = {
    message_delivered_to_application: processingResult.deliveredToApp,
    application_response: processingResult.appResponse,
    notification_sent: processingResult.notificationSent
  };

  // Sign the acknowledgment
  const ackPayload = { ...baseAck, processingMetadata, validationResults, deliveryConfirmation };
  const signature = await signAcknowledgment(ackPayload);

  return {
    ...ackPayload,
    signature
  };
}

docs.title: Signature Verification
docs.text:
async function verifyAcknowledgmentSignature(
  acknowledgment: Acknowledgment,
  senderPublicKey: Uint8Array
): Promise<boolean> {
  const { signature, ...ackWithoutSignature } = acknowledgment;

  // Canonicalize the acknowledgment payload
  const canonicalized = canonicalizeJson(ackWithoutSignature);

  // Verify signature
  return await ed25519Verify(
    canonicalized,
    base64Decode(signature),
    senderPublicKey
  );
}

docs.title: Content Validation
docs.text:
function validateAcknowledgmentContent(acknowledgment: Acknowledgment): boolean {
  // Validate required fields
  const requiredFields = ['id', 'type', 'timestamp', 'original_message_id', 'status'];
  const hasRequiredFields = requiredFields.every(field => field in acknowledgment);

  if (!hasRequiredFields) {
    return false;
  }

  // Validate timestamp
  if (!isValidRFC3339(acknowledgment.timestamp)) {
    return false;
  }

  // Validate status
  const validStatuses = ['delivered', 'verified', 'pending', 'failed'];
  if (!validStatuses.includes(acknowledgment.status)) {
    return false;
  }

  // Validate processing metadata if present
  if (acknowledgment.processing_metadata) {
    if (!validateProcessingMetadata(acknowledgment.processing_metadata)) {
      return false;
    }
  }

  return true;
}

docs.title: Retry Logic Integration
docs.text:
Acknowledgments prevent unnecessary retries and provide feedback for optimization:

class RetryManager {
  private retryQueue = new Map<string, RetryItem>();

  handleAcknowledgment(acknowledgment: Acknowledgment): void {
    const originalMessageId = acknowledgment.original_message_id;

    if (acknowledgment.status === 'delivered' || acknowledgment.status === 'verified') {
      // Remove from retry queue
      this.retryQueue.delete(originalMessageId);

      // Update delivery statistics
      this.updateDeliveryStats(acknowledgment);
    } else if (acknowledgment.status === 'failed') {
      // Handle failed acknowledgment
      this.handleFailedAcknowledgment(acknowledgment);
    }
  }

  private handleFailedAcknowledgment(acknowledgment: Acknowledgment): void {
    const retryItem = this.retryQueue.get(acknowledgment.original_message_id);

    if (retryItem && acknowledgment.error_details?.retryable) {
      // Schedule retry with backoff
      this.scheduleRetry(retryItem);
    } else {
      // Permanent failure
      this.markAsFailed(acknowledgment);
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Delivery Metrics
docs.text:
interface DeliveryMetrics {
  totalMessages: number;
  deliveredMessages: number;
  verifiedMessages: number;
  failedMessages: number;
  averageLatency: number;
  deliveryRate: number;
  retryRate: number;
}

class DeliveryAnalytics {
  private metrics: DeliveryMetrics = {
    totalMessages: 0,
    deliveredMessages: 0,
    verifiedMessages: 0,
    failedMessages: 0,
    averageLatency: 0,
    deliveryRate: 0,
    retryRate: 0
  };

  recordAcknowledgment(acknowledgment: Acknowledgment): void {
    this.metrics.totalMessages++;

    switch (acknowledgment.status) {
      case 'delivered':
        this.metrics.deliveredMessages++;
        break;
      case 'verified':
        this.metrics.verifiedMessages++;
        this.metrics.deliveredMessages++;
        break;
      case 'failed':
        this.metrics.failedMessages++;
        break;
    }

    // Update latency calculations
    if (acknowledgment.processing_metadata?.processing_time_ms) {
      this.updateAverageLatency(acknowledgment.processing_metadata.processing_time_ms);
    }

    // Update rates
    this.updateRates();
  }
}

docs.title: Appendix E: Reference Protocols and Standards

// Example BIP39 implementation

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: The architecture integrates modular cryptographic and messaging components while preserving the ability to interoperate across protocols, chains, and environments. The system enforces secure communication and verifiable identity handling through an abstracted credential model and adaptive transport layer.

docs.title: Identity Layer
docs.text:
• BIP39-compliant seed input to derive deterministic seckp256k1 keypair
  • BIP47-compatible paycode for privacy-preserving address aliasing
  • Globally unique sfname mapped to public credential metadata

docs.title: Credential Graph Layer
docs.text:
• seckp256k1-signed master credentials to create verifiable trust chains
  • Authority signs core identity credentials
  • ContactCredential (formerly NymNetwork) contains peer-verifiable identity claims

docs.title: Messaging & Signaling Layer
docs.text:
• Multimodal encrypted signal transport: Nym Route, On-chain pointer, Beacon, Backup
  • Routes outbound messages using optimal StrategyContext plugin selection
  • Recipients validate and decrypt messages using sender/recipient channel address

docs.title: Payment Trigger Layer
docs.text:
• Monitors signed message events to conditionally trigger blockchain-native payments
  • Integrates with programmable wallet functions (e.g., sendToResolvedPaycode())
  • Includes callback or webhook logic for confirming state change after off-chain handshake

docs.title: Recovery & Replay Layer
docs.text:
• Stores encrypted signal backups with timestamp-based recovery
  • Rebuilds contact from credential chain
  • Fallback: beacon scanning with payload hash match

docs.title: Functional Responsibilities by Module
docs.text: Module Responsibility Identity Layer Key derivation, sfname registration, paycode generation Credential Graph Trust chain creation, credential signing, revocation Messaging Layer Signal routing, encryption, delivery confirmation Payment Layer Transaction monitoring, wallet integration, callbacks Recovery Layer Backup storage, timestamp recovery, beacon scanning

docs.title: Sequence Flow: Wallet Init → Credential Sign → Message
docs.text:
Nym-specific Notes:

  • The routing strategy selected in StrategyContext will invoke Nym-based delivery for high-privacy scenarios or fallback to beacon/on-chain broadcast depending on real-time strategy evaluation.

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text:
• Privacy-first: Nym-based onion routing and credential obfuscation by default
  • Verifiability: All credentials and contact chains are cryptographically signed and inspectable
  • Recoverability: Deterministic BIP39-based recovery plus JSON timestamped signal replay
  • Extensibility: Modules (e.g., Credential Graph, Signal Strategy, Payment Layer) are pluggable and upgradeable
  • Compliance-compatible: Allows selective identity reveal for KYC, DAO voting, or custom logic
  • Interoperability-first: Payment hooks and message structure work across Ethereum, Algorand, and Solana ecosystems

docs.title: Identity & Credential Structure
docs.text:
The system's decentralized identity layer is built upon deterministic cryptographic key derivation (via BIP39 and BIP47), anchored by a secure naming and credentialing system. Each participant in the network is assigned a unique, verifiable identity composed of:

  • Mnemonic Seed (BIP39)
  • Public/Private Keypair (seckp256k1)
  • Sovereign Name Identifier (sfname)
  • Paycode (BIP47-compatible)
  • Signed Contact Credential (optional)

docs.title: Wallet Identity
docs.text:
When a user imports or generates a wallet:

  • A 12-24 word mnemonic is used to deterministically derive the master seed.
  • This seed is transformed into a root keypair using seckp256k1.
  • The sfname is registered as a globally unique human-readable handle.
  • A paycode is derived from the public key using the BIP47 protocol.

Example Wallet Identity Object:

{
  "mnemonic": "abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon about",
  "master_seed": "0x...",
  "keypair": {
    "public_key": "0x0279BE667EF9DCBBAC55A06295CE870B07029BFCDB2DCE28D959F2815B16F81798",
    "private_key": "0x...",
    "compressed": true
  },
  "sfname": "alice.wallet",
  "paycode": "PM8TJXuZ63i8AuSBLVUuqVQt3HFPwz2KGPNWJ3K6WrEZFqeJVZBHU4CQVYLK8He",
  "created_at": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z"
}

docs.title: Contact Credential
docs.text:
A Contact Credential is a signed payload representing the linking of one wallet's public identity to another. It enables:

  • Mutual key discovery and relationship acknowledgment
  • Session recovery and replay prevention
  • In-message authorization and payment signaling

Each Contact Credential contains:

  • Issuer sfname and public key
  • Recipient sfname and public key
  • Issuance timestamp
  • seckp256k1 signature of the payload

Example Contact Credential Payload:

{
  "type": "ContactCredential",
  "version": "1.0",
  "issuer": {
    "sfname": "alice.wallet",
    "pubkey": "0x0279BE667EF9DCBBAC55A06295CE870B07029BFCDB2DCE28D959F2815B16F81798"
  },
  "recipient": {
    "sfname": "bob.wallet",
    "pubkey": "0x03B9A7E9C3D4E5F6A7B8C9D0E1F2A3B4C5D6E7F8A9B0C1D2E3F4A5B6C7D8E9F0"
  },
  "issued_at": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z",
  "metadata": {
    "relationship": "friend",
    "notes": "Regular trading partner"
  },
  "signature": "0x..."
}

docs.title: Authority Signature (Nym System)
docs.text:
To validate identity origin and ensure protection against impersonation, each identity is optionally backed by a Nym Authority signature. This signature:

  • Confirms linkage to a verifiable messaging node (signal relayer)
  • Can be validated offline
  • Enables private routing enforcement policies

Nym Authority Proof Structure:

{
  "authority_id": "nym-authority-001",
  "identity_proof": {
    "sfname": "alice.wallet",
    "nym_address": "nym://alice@relay.nymtech.net",
    "proof_type": "nym_binding"
  },
  "issued_at": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z",
  "valid_until": "2026-08-18T10:00:00Z",
  "signature": "0x..."
}

docs.title: Contact Graph Representation
docs.text:
Internally, all contact credentials form a directed acyclic graph (DAG) stored in the user's local WalletSDK cache and replicated selectively via encrypted backup channels.

Each node = sfname Each edge = Signed ContactCredential

docs.title: Revocation & Expiry
docs.text:
Credentials may include TTL (time-to-live) headers or be manually revoked. WalletSDK will prune expired or revoked credentials from the graph to maintain consistency.

Revocation Object:

{
  "type": "CredentialRevocation",
  "credential_id": "cred_12345",
  "revoked_by": "alice.wallet",
  "revoked_at": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z",
  "reason": "contact_terminated",
  "signature": "0x..."
}

docs.title: Paycode Write and Verification (DeFi Anchor)
docs.text:
Upon successful POST /identity/register, the system performs the following:

Write to DeFi Signaling Contract:

// Smart contract call
await signalingContract.registerIdentity(
  sfname,
  paycode,
  pubkey,
  signature
);

This event acts as the canonical record of identity registration.

SDK Verification Flow:

// Verify on-chain registration
const events = await signalingContract.queryFilter(
  signalingContract.filters.IdentityRegistered(sfname)
);

if (events.length > 0) {
  // Registration confirmed
  updateLocalState({ identityConfirmed: true });
}

This ensures the on-chain registration was successful and links: sfname → paycode → pubkey.

Fallback Logic: If verification fails (e.g. no event found), the SDK:

  • Displays a retry UI for re-registration
  • Logs event in localAuditTrail()
  • Flags the state as identityNotConfirmed

docs.title: Technical Specifications Document
docs.text:
Version: 1.2

Date: August 18th, 2025

Prepared by: Javier Calderon / Mehow / Bartek

The AFSDK leverages cryptographic primitives and zero-trust design principles to enable secure, private communication and identity assertion between wallet users on-chain and off-chain. This specification provides a unified interface for applications integrating MatterFi's AFSDK and adaptation of Nym, a privacy-enhancing identity model.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Self-sovereign identity derived from deterministic cryptographic seed (BIP39)
  • Encrypted off-chain communication using secure channels over WebSocket or HTTP
  • Verifiable credentials through structured Authority and ContactCredential
  • Multimodal signaling supporting dynamic fallback paths for delivery and recovery
  • KYC-compatible privacy enabling users to share verifiable data without compromising anonymity

docs.title: Target Audience
docs.text: This specification targets full-stack developers, cryptographic engineers, and systems integrators building user-centric blockchain and privacy-first Web3 applications.

docs.title: Document Structure
docs.text:
This documentation contains the complete technical specification organized into the following chapters:

  1. Introduction - Overview and capabilities
  2. Architecture Overview - System design and tiers
  3. API Endpoints - HTTP and WebSocket endpoints
  4. Authentication Flow - Session management and security
  5. Identity & Credential Structure - Identity management
  6. Strategy Pattern - Routing strategies
  7. Socket Parameters & Signal Routing - Real-time communication
  8. Encryption Logic - Cryptographic framework
  9. Beaconing, Discovery, and Recovery - Message discovery
  10. Error and Retry Handling - Reliability mechanisms
  11. Appendices - Reference materials and examples

docs.title: Strategy Pattern
docs.text: The AFSDK implements the Strategy Pattern for dynamic routing of encrypted signals across multiple transport channels. This design allows the system to select optimal routing strategies at runtime based on network conditions, message priority, and security requirements.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Routing strategies are selected using SignalRouterStrategy, which applies the Strategy Pattern to determine the most appropriate transport mechanism for outbound messages. The strategy selection considers factors such as:

  • Message priority and size
  • Network availability and latency
  • Privacy requirements
  • Cost optimization
  • Geographic routing preferences

docs.title: Strategy Context
docs.text:
The StrategyContext serves as the main entry point for message routing decisions:

interface StrategyContext {
  evaluate(message: SignalMessage): Promise<RoutingStrategy>;
  execute(strategy: RoutingStrategy, message: SignalMessage): Promise<RoutingResult>;
}

docs.title: Available Routing Strategies
docs.text: Strategy Description Use Case NymRoute Onion routing through Nym mixnet High-privacy scenarios DirectChannel Peer-to-peer WebSocket connection Low-latency, trusted peers BeaconBroadcast On-chain beacon signaling Decentralized discovery BackupRelay Encrypted backup storage Message persistence HybridFallback Combination of multiple strategies Reliability-focused routing

docs.title: SignalRouter Strategy Implementation
docs.text:
abstract class RoutingStrategy {
  abstract name: string;
  abstract priority: number;

  abstract canHandle(message: SignalMessage): boolean;
  abstract execute(message: SignalMessage): Promise<RoutingResult>;
}

class SignalRouterStrategy {
  private strategies: RoutingStrategy[] = [];

  registerStrategy(strategy: RoutingStrategy): void {
    this.strategies.push(strategy);
    this.strategies.sort((a, b) => b.priority - a.priority);
  }

  async route(message: SignalMessage): Promise<RoutingResult> {
    for (const strategy of this.strategies) {
      if (strategy.canHandle(message)) {
        try {
          return await strategy.execute(message);
        } catch (error) {
          console.warn(`Strategy ${strategy.name} failed:`, error);
          continue;
        }
      }
    }

    throw new Error('No suitable routing strategy found');
  }
}

docs.title: Dynamic Routing Logic
docs.text: Signal routing may dynamically select from available channels based on real-time conditions:

docs.title: Primary Selection Criteria
docs.text:
1. Privacy Level: Nym routing for maximum privacy
  2. Latency Requirements: Direct channels for real-time communication
  3. Network Availability: Fallback to available alternatives
  4. Cost Optimization: Balance between privacy and efficiency

docs.title: Fallback Chain
docs.text: Nym Route → Direct WebSocket → Beacon Broadcast → Backup Storage

docs.title: Strategy Evaluation Flow
docs.text:
class StrategyEvaluator {
  async evaluateStrategies(message: SignalMessage): Promise<RoutingStrategy[]> {
    const availableStrategies = await this.getAvailableStrategies();
    const scoredStrategies = await Promise.all(
      availableStrategies.map(async (strategy) => ({
        strategy,
        score: await this.scoreStrategy(strategy, message)
      }))
    );

    return scoredStrategies
      .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
      .map(item => item.strategy);
  }

  private async scoreStrategy(strategy: RoutingStrategy, message: SignalMessage): Promise<number> {
    let score = strategy.priority;

    // Adjust score based on message characteristics
    if (message.priority === 'high' && strategy.name === 'DirectChannel') {
      score += 10;
    }

    // Adjust score based on network conditions
    const networkCondition = await this.getNetworkCondition();
    if (networkCondition.latency > 1000 && strategy.name === 'BeaconBroadcast') {
      score -= 5;
    }

    return score;
  }
}

docs.title: Runtime Strategy Selection
docs.text:
The system supports dynamic strategy switching based on:

  • Network Conditions: Automatic fallback when primary routes fail
  • Message Priority: High-priority messages may bypass slower but more private routes
  • User Preferences: Configurable routing policies
  • Security Context: Enhanced privacy for sensitive communications

docs.title: Strategy Configuration
docs.text:
Strategies can be configured at runtime through the SDK options:

const sdkConfig = {
  routing: {
    preferredStrategies: ['nym', 'direct'],
    fallbackEnabled: true,
    maxRetries: 3,
    timeoutMs: 5000
  }
};

docs.title: Monitoring and Analytics
docs.text:
Each strategy execution is logged for monitoring and optimization:

interface RoutingResult {
  strategy: string;
  success: boolean;
  duration: number;
  bytesTransferred: number;
  error?: string;
}

This data helps improve strategy selection over time and provides insights into routing performance across different network conditions.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The key design goal is to establish a many-to-one mapping, where multiple users' payment codes deterministically map to the same beacon identifier, forming logical "buckets." This enables users to efficiently identify which encrypted messages in a shared inbox are intended for them — without needing to decrypt full payloads. The scheme combines asymmetric encryption, cryptographic tagging, and a lightweight header, all of which can be processed using only the recipient's private key.

This design is particularly useful in decentralized messaging systems, wallet-based notification protocols, and off-chain asynchronous communication built on top of blockchain infrastructure.

To enhance the OBPP-05 specification and support more scalable messaging in decentralized networks, we propose an algorithm that introduces shared beacon identifiers instead of assigning a unique one per user. This allows multiple recipients to monitor a single beacon address for relevant messages.

In this context, a beacon acts as a detectable on-chain or off-chain marker that lets recipients infer whether a message might be intended for them — without revealing their identity until decryption.

docs.title: Key Benefits
docs.text:
• Fewer unique scripts or notification addresses to monitor
  • Improved performance for lightweight clients using mechanisms like compact filters (e.g cfilters)
  • Compatibility with wallet-to-wallet notification protocols

docs.title: Proposed Algorithm (Enhancement of OBPP-05)
docs.text:
Compute beacon_id

Let:

  • p be the compressed public key from the recipient's payment code,
  • v be the 1-byte version of the sender's payment code (as per OBPP-05 compatibility).

Steps:

  1. Compute a "chain code" from the recipient key:
chain_code = HMAC-SHA512(p, "beacon-chain-code")
  1. Generate HMAC using sender version:
hmac_result = HMAC-SHA512(chain_code, v)
  1. Truncate the HMAC to M bytes to produce a beacon identifier:
beacon_id = hmac_result.substring(0, M)

Then such a beacon_id can be used as a group identifier in smart_contract collection.

docs.title: Message Structure (Hybrid Encryption Mode)
docs.text:
Each message includes the following fields:

Field Description Size beacon_id Group identifier M bytes tag Encrypted nonce for filtering \~100 bytes hash_nonce SHA256 of nonce 32 bytes payload Encrypted paycode Variable metadata Routing information Variable

The protocol uses a lightweight tag mechanism instead of hybrid AES. The payload is small (an encrypted paycode), so asymmetric encryption is sufficient and avoids unnecessary complexity. The tag allows recipients to filter inboxes efficiently before attempting payload decryption.

docs.title: Sender-Side Process
docs.text:
1. Generate a 128-bit random nonce N.
const nonce = crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(16));
  1. Compute:
const tag = encryptWithPublicKey(nonce, recipientPublicKey);
const hashNonce = sha256(nonce);
  1. Encrypt the paycode payload directly with the recipient's public key (OBPP-5 style).
  2. Construct message:
{
  "beacon_id": "beacon_12345",
  "tag": "encrypted_nonce_here",
  "hash_nonce": "sha256_hash_here",
  "payload": "encrypted_paycode_here",
  "timestamp": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z"
}

docs.title: Recipient-Side Process
docs.text:
For each message in the inbox:

  1. Decrypt tag with their private key to recover nonce N'
  2. Verify SHA256(N') == hash_nonce
  3. Match → proceed
  4. Mismatch → ignore message
  5. Decrypt payload using private key to recover the paycode.

docs.title: Pseudocode Example
docs.text:
Sender:

function createBeaconMessage(senderPaycode, recipientPaycode, message) {
  // Generate beacon ID
  const beaconId = computeBeaconId(recipientPaycode.publicKey, senderPaycode.version);

  // Create filtering tag
  const nonce = generateRandomNonce();
  const tag = encryptWithPublicKey(nonce, recipientPaycode.publicKey);
  const hashNonce = sha256(nonce);

  // Encrypt payload
  const encryptedPayload = encryptWithPublicKey(message, recipientPaycode.publicKey);

  return {
    beacon_id: beaconId,
    tag: tag,
    hash_nonce: hashNonce,
    payload: encryptedPayload
  };
}

Recipient:

function processBeaconMessage(message, privateKey) {
  // Try to decrypt tag
  try {
    const decryptedNonce = decryptWithPrivateKey(message.tag, privateKey);
    const computedHash = sha256(decryptedNonce);

    // Check if this message is for us
    if (computedHash !== message.hash_nonce) {
      return { relevant: false };
    }

    // Decrypt the actual payload
    const decryptedPayload = decryptWithPrivateKey(message.payload, privateKey);

    return {
      relevant: true,
      payload: decryptedPayload,
      beacon_id: message.beacon_id
    };
  } catch (error) {
    return { relevant: false };
  }
}

docs.title: End-to-End Scenario: Bob Sends Notification to Alice
docs.text:
Actors:

  • Alice: Public key PK_A, Private key SK_A
  • Bob: Wants to send Alice a private notification: "Bob_pcode" (payload)

Bob's Process:

  1. Bob Generates Random nonce (16 bytes)

  2. Bob Prepares the Header:

    • tag = EncryptWithPublicKey(N, PK_A)
    • hash_nonce = SHA256(N)

    Example:

    {
  "tag": "encrypted_nonce_data",
  "hash_nonce": "a665a45920422f9d417e4867efdc4fb8a04a1f3fff1fa07e998e86f7f7a27ae3"
}
    3. Bob Encrypts the payload 4. Bob Constructs the Message:

    {
  "beacon_id": "beacon_alice_group",
  "tag": "encrypted_nonce_data",
  "hash_nonce": "a665a45920422f9d417e4867efdc4fb8a04a1f3fff1fa07e998e86f7f7a27ae3",
  "payload": "encrypted_bob_paycode"
}

Alice's Process:

  1. Decrypt the tag:
  2. N' = DecryptWithPrivateKey(tag, SK_A)
  3. Hash the result:
  4. hash_check = SHA256(N')
  5. Compare:
  6. If hash_check != hash_nonce, discard the message
  7. Otherwise, continue
  8. Decrypt the payload:
  9. If match → decrypts payload with SK_A → obtains Bob's paycode
  10. Result: Bob_pcode

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Simplicity: avoids the unnecessary AES layer
  • Efficiency: one small asymmetric decryption per message for filtering
  • Compatibility: reuses OBPP-5 signal encryption
  • Security: only the intended recipient can recognize and decrypt the message

docs.title: Advantages
docs.text:
• Scalability: Each message requires only one small asymmetric decryption of the tag for filtering, making the system efficient even for large shared inboxes
  • Security: Only the intended recipient can validate and decrypt the payload; the hash_nonce check ensures tags cannot be forged
  • Simplicity: Eliminates the complexity of hybrid AES schemes — the entire mechanism relies solely on well-understood public-key cryptography
  • Robustness: The tag+hash mechanism prevents false positives and ensures that recipients never waste resources attempting to decrypt irrelevant payloads

docs.title: Performance in Very Large Inboxes
docs.text:
Challenge: Repeated asymmetric tag decryptions may still become costly.

Mitigation: Use elliptic-curve-based encryption (e.g., ECIES) for faster operations, or batch-process tag checks in parallel.

docs.title: Header Size
docs.text:
Challenge: Even with just tag + hash, the header introduces overhead (≈100-150 bytes with ECC).

Mitigation: Optimize tag length or use compact encodings to minimize bandwidth/storage impact.

docs.title: Denial-of-Service (DoS)
docs.text:
Challenge: An attacker could flood inboxes with fake messages, forcing recipients to decrypt many tags.

Mitigation: Add inbox-level rate-limiting, lightweight metadata filtering, or reputation-based throttling before attempting asymmetric decryption.

docs.title: Key Derivation Tags
docs.text:
Instead of encrypting a nonce, derive a tag from the recipient's public key using a key derivation function (KDF). For example:

T = KDF(PK_i, sender_secret)

The recipient verifies the tag using their private key and the sender's public key. This avoids an extra encryption but requires careful KDF design to prevent key leakage.

docs.title: Backup Smart Contract System
docs.text:
Backup smart contract provides a secure, privacy-preserving way for wallets to store and recover lists of counterparty paycodes.

Main Design Goals:

  • Unlinkability: The backup inbox must not be traceable to the user's public payment code
  • Write Protection: Only the owner wallet can write to their backup inbox
  • Privacy & Security: Data is encrypted; no one else can read or write to the inbox

Use Case:

  1. Alice decrypts notifications from the beacon
  2. She gets Bob's paycode as a signal sender
  3. Alice encrypts Bob's paycode and appends it to her backup inbox
  4. Later, if Alice restores her wallet from seed, she derives backup private key again, reads her encrypted inbox, and decrypts her counterparty list

docs.title: Encryption Logic
docs.text: This section outlines the cryptographic framework used to ensure the integrity, privacy, and non-repudiation of all communications, credentials, and payments within the WalletSDK.

docs.title: Key Hierarchy and Derivation
docs.text:
All cryptographic operations begin with a BIP39-compliant mnemonic, from which the following key hierarchy is deterministically derived:

  • Master Key (BIP32 root): Root of trust, never exposed directly
// Master key derivation from mnemonic
const masterKey = deriveMasterKeyFromMnemonic(mnemonic);
  • Messaging Key (seckp256k1): Used for message signature and X25519 conversion
  • Credential Key (seckp256k1): Dedicated to ContactCredential signing
  • Paycode Key (BIP47): Derived separately for Paycode-based signaling
  • Session Keys: Ephemeral seckp256k1 keys rotated per conversation context

These keys form the basis for secure signaling, credential issuance, and verification.

docs.title: Core Cryptographic Algorithms
docs.text:
Encryption is implemented using a hybrid scheme:

  • Elliptic Curve: seckp256k1 for signing, X25519 for Diffie-Hellman key exchange
  • KDF: HKDF-SHA512 with context-specific salt
  • Symmetric Cipher: AES-256-GCM with 96-bit nonce and AAD
  • Signature Scheme: seckp256k1 (RFC 8032)

docs.title: Message Encryption Workflow
docs.text:
All messages follow a standard ECIES process adapted to our X25519 + AES-GCM model:

  1. Sender generates ephemeral seckp256k1 keypair
  2. Sender derives shared secret via X25519(ephemeral, recipient_pub)
  3. Derive AES-GCM key via HKDF(shared_secret, context_info)
  4. Encrypt message payload using AES-GCM with fresh nonce
  5. Sign the encrypted payload using seckp256k1
  6. Construct payload with:
  7. ephemeral_pub
  8. nonce
  9. ciphertext
  10. signature

docs.title: Message Decryption Workflow
docs.text:
async function decryptMessage(
  encryptedMessage: EncryptedMessage,
  recipientPrivateKey: Uint8Array
): Promise<string> {
  // Extract components
  const { ephemeralPub, nonce, ciphertext, signature } = encryptedMessage;

  // Derive shared secret
  const sharedSecret = x25519(recipientPrivateKey, ephemeralPub);

  // Regenerate AES-GCM key
  const aesKey = hkdf(sharedSecret, 'message-encryption', 32);

  // Decrypt ciphertext
  const plaintext = aesGcmDecrypt(ciphertext, aesKey, nonce);

  // Verify signature
  const messageHash = sha256(ciphertext);
  const isValid = secp256k1.verify(messageHash, signature, ephemeralPub);

  if (!isValid) {
    throw new Error('Invalid signature');
  }

  return plaintext;
}

docs.title: Credential Signing and Nym Binding
docs.text:
Credential issuance follows a canonical signing pattern:

Each credential is signed by the Nym's Master Key, and the resulting signature is bundled with the credential itself. The Nym ID is computed as:

const nymId = sha256(masterPublicKey + authorityProof);

This allows any verifier to compute and trust the provenance of a credential using only the public key and signature.

docs.title: Nym Structure for Credential Authority
docs.text:
Nyms contain credentials bundled in a verified Authority object:

{
  "nym_id": "nym_12345",
  "authority": {
    "public_key": "0x...",
    "credentials": [
      {
        "type": "ContactCredential",
        "signature": "0x...",
        "issued_at": "2025-08-18T10:00:00Z"
      }
    ]
  },
  "trust_chain": ["authority_1", "authority_2"]
}

This structure binds cryptographic claims and public key lineage, ensuring trust anchors are established securely.

docs.title: Session Encryption (Optional)
docs.text:
Where Double Ratchet encryption is used for live chat or streaming signals:

  • Ratchets derived per session and per direction
  • Forward secrecy on message level
  • Replay-resistant with nonces per message
class DoubleRatchetSession {
  private rootKey: Uint8Array;
  private sendingChain: RatchetChain;
  private receivingChain: RatchetChain;

  async encryptMessage(plaintext: string): Promise<EncryptedMessage> {
    const { key, index } = this.sendingChain.nextKey();
    const ciphertext = aesGcmEncrypt(plaintext, key);
    const signature = this.signMessage(ciphertext);

    return {
      ciphertext,
      signature,
      chainIndex: index,
      ephemeralKey: this.sendingChain.publicKey
    };
  }
}

docs.title: Tamper & Replay Protection
docs.text:
Each message includes the following anti-tamper layers:

  • Encrypted nonce + timestamp
  • Signature over full payload and ephemeral key
  • Local hash cache to detect replays
  • Optional Nym chain inclusion for credential replay state

These guarantees enforce strict sender authenticity, integrity of messages, and revocation-awareness without introducing unnecessary on-chain dependencies.

docs.text: This diagram shows both the encryption lifecycle from sender to recipient and the verification of cryptographic claims (NymID) through credential signature validation.

docs.title: Data Structures
docs.text:
// Unified error codes (mirroring C API)
const ErrorCodes = {
  SUCCESS: 0,
  INTERNAL_ERROR: 1,
  RECIPIENT_INVALID: 2,
  NOT_FOUND: 4,
  ADDRESS_CALCULATING_ERROR: 5,
  ADDRESS_CONVERSION_ERROR: 6,
  INVALID_CLAIM: 7,
  INVALID_ACCOUNT: 8,
  INVALID_SEED: 9,
  INVALID_RECIPIENT: 10,
  INVALID_SFNAME: 11,
  SFNAME_IN_USE: 12,
  INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS: 13,
  FEE_TOO_LOW: 14,
  TRANSFER_FAILED: 15,
  PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND: 16,
  PAYMENT_CANNOT_CANCEL: 17,
  INVALID_TOKEN: 18,
  STAKE_TOO_LOW = 19,
  UNSTAKE_NOT_ALLOWED = 20,
  VALIDATOR_NOT_FOUND = 21
};

// Unified result structure (mirroring C API)
class MatterFiResult {
  constructor(error, data) {
    this.error = error;
    this.data = data;
  }

  isSuccess() {
    return this.error === ErrorCodes.SUCCESS;
  }

  getData() {
    return this.data;
  }

  getErrorMessage() {
    return Object.keys(ErrorCodes).find(key => ErrorCodes[key] === this.error) || 'UNKNOWN_ERROR';
  }
}

const MatterFi = require('matterfi-sdk');

// Initialize SDK
await MatterFi.init('mywallet', JSON.stringify({ networks: ['bitcoin'] }));

// Generate seed
const seedResult = MatterFi.seedGenerate();
if (seedResult.isSuccess()) {
  console.log('Recovery words:', seedResult.getData());
} else {
  console.error('Error:', seedResult.getErrorMessage());
}

// Import existing seed
const importResult = MatterFi.seedImport(words, '', 0, 0);
if (!importResult.isSuccess()) {
  console.error('Import failed:', importResult.getErrorMessage());
}

// Create identity
const nymResult = MatterFi.createNym(words, 'password', 'alice');
if (nymResult.isSuccess()) {
  console.log('Identity created successfully');
}

// Create account
const accountResult = MatterFi.makeAccount('bitcoin');
if (accountResult.isSuccess()) {
  const accountId = accountResult.getData();

  // Check balance
  const balanceResult = MatterFi.getBalance(0, accountId); // 0 = bitcoin chain
  if (balanceResult.isSuccess()) {
    console.log('Balance:', balanceResult.getData());
  }

  // Send payment

  if (feeResult.isSuccess()) {

    if (paymentResult.isSuccess()) {
      console.log('Payment sent:', paymentResult.getData());
    }
  }
}

// Cleanup
MatterFi.shutdown();

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
// Error handling with unified result structure
const seedResult = MatterFi.seedImport("bad words", "", 0, 0);
if (!seedResult.isSuccess()) {
  console.error(`Import failed: ${seedResult.getErrorMessage()}`);
}

// Multiple operation example with proper error handling
async function performWalletOperations() {
  try {
    // Generate seed
    const seedResult = MatterFi.seedGenerate();
    if (!seedResult.isSuccess()) {
      throw new Error(`Seed generation failed: ${seedResult.getErrorMessage()}`);
    }
    const words = seedResult.getData();

    // Import seed
    const importResult = MatterFi.seedImport(words, '', 0, 0);
    if (!importResult.isSuccess()) {
      throw new Error(`Seed import failed: ${importResult.getErrorMessage()}`);
    }

    // Create identity
    const nymResult = MatterFi.createNym(words, 'password', 'alice');
    if (!nymResult.isSuccess()) {
      throw new Error(`Identity creation failed: ${nymResult.getErrorMessage()}`);
    }

    // Create account
    const accountResult = MatterFi.makeAccount('bitcoin');
    if (!accountResult.isSuccess()) {
      throw new Error(`Account creation failed: ${accountResult.getErrorMessage()}`);
    }

    console.log('All operations completed successfully!');
    return accountResult.getData();

  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Wallet operation failed:', error.message);
    return null;
  }
}

docs.title: Memory Management
docs.text: The SDK handles memory management automatically. JavaScript objects are managed by the JS runtime, while C strings are freed by the native bindings.

docs.title: Data Structures
docs.text:
// Unified error codes for all API operations 

typedef enum {
    ...
    MATTERFI_BEACON_SUCCESS = 100,
    MATTERFI_BEACON_INTERNAL_ERROR = 101,
    MATTERFI_BEACON_RECIPIENT_INVALID = 102,
    MATTERFI_BEACON_NOT_FOUND = 103
} matterfi_error_t;

// Unified result structure for all API responses
typedef struct {
    matterfi_error_t error;
    char* data;  // Generic data field (string, JSON, etc.) - ownership transferred
} matterfi_result_t;

// Compute beacon ID for a given recipient public key
// my_payment_code: Payment code of the user whose beacon ID we want to compute
// Returns: matterfi_result_t containing:
//   - error: MATTERFI_BEACON_SUCCESS if OK, or [MATTERFI_BEACON_INTERNAL_ERROR, MATTERFI_BEACON_RECIPIENT_INVALID] on failure
//   - data: Beacon ID string (ownership transferred, must be freed with matterfi_string_free) 
matterfi_result_t matterfi_beacon_get_my_id(const char* my_payment_code);

// Send a notification to a recipient
// from_payment_code: Sender's payment code
// to_payment_code: Recipient's payment code
// payload: Message or payload to send
// Returns: matterfi_result_t containing:
//   - error: MATTERFI_BEACON_SUCCESS on success, or [MATTERFI_BEACON_INTERNAL_ERROR, MATTERFI_BEACON_RECIPIENT_INVALID] on failure
//   - data: Optional data or NULL
matterfi_result_t matterfi_beacon_notify(const char* from_payment_code, const char* to_payment_code, const char* payload);

// Receive notifications for a given beacon
// beacon_id: Beacon identifier for which to fetch messages
// Returns: matterfi_result_t containing:
//   - error: MATTERFI_BEACON_SUCCESS if OK, or [MATTERFI_BEACON_INTERNAL_ERROR, MATTERFI_BEACON_NOT_FOUND] on failure

docs.title: Data Structures
docs.text:
// Unified error codes for all API operations
typedef enum {
    MATTERFI_SUCCESS = 0,
    MATTERFI_INTERNAL_ERROR = 1,
    MATTERFI_RECIPIENT_INVALID = 2,
    MATTERFI_NOT_FOUND = 4,
    MATTERFI_ADDRESS_CALCULATING_ERROR = 5,
    MATTERFI_ADDRESS_CONVERSION_ERROR = 6,
    MATTERFI_INVALID_CLAIM = 7,
    MATTERFI_INVALID_ACCOUNT = 8,
    MATTERFI_INVALID_SEED = 9,
    MATTERFI_INVALID_RECIPIENT = 10,
    MATTERFI_INVALID_SFNAME = 11,
    MATTERFI_SFNAME_IN_USE = 12,
    MATTERFI_INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS = 13,
    MATTERFI_FEE_TOO_LOW = 14,
    MATTERFI_TRANSFER_FAILED = 15,
    MATTERFI_PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND = 16,
    MATTERFI_PAYMENT_CANNOT_CANCEL = 17,
    MATTERFI_INVALID_TOKEN = 18,
    MATTERFI_STAKE_TOO_LOW = 19,
    MATTERFI_UNSTAKE_NOT_ALLOWED = 20,
    MATTERFI_VALIDATOR_NOT_FOUND = 21
} matterfi_error_t;

// Unified result structure for all API responses
typedef struct {
    matterfi_error_t error;
    char* data;  // Generic data field (string, JSON, etc.) - ownership transferred
} matterfi_result_t;

typedef enum {
    MATTERFI_ETH,
    MATTERFI_SOL
} matterfi_network_t;

#define MATTERFI_CHAIN_TESTNET     1    // Testnets (all networks)

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All API functions return a matterfi_result_t structure containing:

  • error: Error code from the unified matterfi_error_t enum (defined above)
  • data: Generic data field containing the result (string, JSON, etc.)

Example usage:

matterfi_result_t result = matterfi_generate_seed();
if (result.error == MATTERFI_SUCCESS) {
    printf("Generated seed: %s\n", result.data);
    matterfi_string_free(result.data); // Free the data
} else {
    printf("Error: %d\n", result.error);
}

docs.title: MatterFi C API Usage Patterns
docs.text: Note: These examples demonstrate usage patterns for the MatterFi C API. They show how the unified result structure pattern works in practice. For actual implementation details, see the C API Bindings documentation.

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text:
• ✅ C API: Available with unified result structures
  • 🚧 JavaScript SDK: These patterns will be used in future JavaScript bindings

Examples of using the MatterFi C API for common wallet operations.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
const MatterFi = require('matterfi-sdk');

// Initialize SDK
await MatterFi.init('mywallet', JSON.stringify({
  networks: ['bitcoin', 'ethereum']
}));

// Generate and import seed
const words = MatterFi.seedGenerate();
const success = MatterFi.seedImport(words, '', 0, 0);

// Create identity
const nymResult = MatterFi.createNym(words, 'password', 'alice');

// Create account
const accountId = MatterFi.makeAccount('bitcoin');

// Get balance
const balance = MatterFi.getBalance(0, accountId);

// Send payment
const fee = MatterFi.calculateFee(0);
const txId = MatterFi.sendPayment(0, accountId, 'recipient', '1000000', fee);

// Cleanup
MatterFi.shutdown();

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
const ErrorCodes = {
  0: 'SUCCESS',
  1: 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
  5: 'ADDRESS_CALCULATING_ERROR',
  6: 'ADDRESS_CONVERSION_ERROR',
  7: 'INVALID_CLAIM',
  8: 'INVALID_ACCOUNT',
  9: 'INVALID_SEED',
  10: 'INVALID_RECIPIENT',
  11: 'INVALID_SFNAME',
  12: 'SFNAME_IN_USE',
  13: 'INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS',
  14: 'FEE_TOO_LOW',
  15: 'TRANSFER_FAILED',
  16: 'PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND',
  17: 'PAYMENT_CANNOT_CANCEL',
  18: 'INVALID_TOKEN',
};

function translateError(code) {
  return ErrorCodes[code] || `UNKNOWN_ERROR_${code}`;
}

// Usage examples
const seedResult = MatterFi.seedImport("invalid words", "", 0, 0);
if (seedResult !== 0) {
  console.error(`Seed import failed: ${translateError(seedResult)}`);
}

const paymentResult = MatterFi.sendPayment(0, accountId, 'invalid', '1000000', '1000');
if (paymentResult === null) {
  console.error('Payment failed - check error codes');
}
